Why Job Was So Great a Sufferer.
title: Why Job Was So Great a Sufferer.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Why Job Was So Great a Sufferer.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: And when he says concerning the Lord, “For many bruises hath He inflicted upon me without a cause,” observe that his words are not, He hath inflicted none with a cause; but, “many without a cause.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For it was not because of his manifold sins that these many bruises were inflicted on him, but in order to make trial of his patience.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For on account of his sins, indeed, without which, as he acknowledges in another passage, he was certainly not, he yet judges that he ought to have suffered less.
